title: Security
intro: A plain-language look at how gpm keeps your secrets safe — and what it does and does not protect against.

local.title: Secrets stay on your device
local.body: gpm keeps a local age-encrypted copy of your repository and syncs over git to a repository you own. No cloud, no third-party servers.

copyShow.title: Copy vs. show
copyShow.body: “Copy password” writes straight from secure memory to the clipboard and never enters the app's interface. “Show” puts the password on screen only to be read, then clears it on a timer, when you navigate away, or when you dismiss it.

autoLock.title: The key is wiped after every use
autoLock.body: By default the unlock key is decrypted for a single operation and erased right after, so it is not left sitting in memory. You can change the timing in Locking & auto-clear.

atRest.title: Encrypted at rest on Android
atRest.body: On Android your identity and repository configuration are sealed with a hardware-backed key from the Keystore. The desktop app keeps these as plain files — a documented difference, since desktop has no equivalent keystore.

appLock.title: Optional App Lock
appLock.body: Turn on App Lock to require your fingerprint or face before anyone can read the store. The key is wiped whenever the app returns to the foreground, so a locked app cannot read it even from memory. Set it up in App Lock settings.

authenticity.title: Optional signature verification
authenticity.body: gpm can verify SSH- or GPG-signed commits on every sync to detect a tampered remote. Turn it on under Repository.

scope.title: What this protects against
scope.body: gpm defends against someone briefly using your unlocked device, or a malicious app injecting script. It is not a defense against a compromised or rooted operating system.
